mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the risk-free discount rate applied to equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 5-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating household income and consumer spending power, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns within the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE increases by over 0.5 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand growth, which sustains corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal fails to generate positive 60-day forward returns in over 60% of historical occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in the index of industrial production during a late-cycle expansion phase triggers immediate profit margin compression fears, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are not significantly negative when INDPRO drops over 1.0% during late-cycle phases, or the signal performs identically across early-cycle and late-cycle regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising core inflation combined with a stagnant federal funds rate forces markets to price in aggressive future tightening, which compresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL increases > 0.3% month-over-month while FEDFUNDS remains flat
failure_condition: The combined signal does not predict negative 20-day returns with a win rate above 50%, or the predictive power vanishes when CPIAUCSL rises independently of FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects a healthy labor market that supports wage growth and consumer demand, systematically driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show a negative or zero correlation with PAYEMS growth over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in real gross domestic product confirms economic contraction, immediately triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 45% of quarters where GDPC1 contracts, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold baseline during contraction periods.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase signals rapid capacity utilization and corporate earnings rebound, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns do not exhibit a statistically significant positive mean when INDPRO accelerates during recovery phases, or the effect size is indistinguishable from random noise (p > 0.10).
